body.title: Router: system prompt is data keyed by domain (coding populated from DS prompt)
body.description:
**Problem:** the system prompt is hardcoded per-caller (DS's builder prompt lives in DS/ToolLoop). Intention-Based Development declares 'the system IS domain-routed for BOTH model and prompt' — so the prompt must be data keyed on domain, not baked into the caller; otherwise adding a domain needs code. **Proposed shape:** introduce a domain->prompt lookup (small data map under devices/inference/) with ONE live entry: coding = the current DS builder/tool-loop system prompt MOVED VERBATIM. DS resolves its system prompt by domain. Per-model prompt adapters are explicitly OUT (note as a future optional descriptor field). **First step — separability check:** confirm the DS 'coding' system prompt is cleanly separable from tool-loop/DONE-envelope mechanics; if tangled, extract the domain-prompt portion cleanly first (that separation IS part of this ticket, and is why 'byte-identical' is scoped to the domain-prompt text, not the whole assembled system message). **Affected files:** a domain-prompt data file under unseen_university/devices/inference/, rules_engine.py or a small prompt resolver, devices/dicksimnel/ (consume domain-resolved prompt). **Design rules:** NO SQLITE; prompt is data keyed by domain; ZERO behavior change for coding. **Scope boundary:** IN: domain->prompt seam with coding populated from the existing DS prompt; DS consumes it by domain; separability extraction if needed. OUT: any NEW domain's prompt, per-model adapters, prompt rewrites. **Test plan (stub-first red->green):** (1) resolving prompt for domain='coding' returns the existing DS builder-prompt text byte-identical to today's; (2) a DS run uses the domain-resolved prompt and still reaches DONE on the smoke fixture; (3) a second stub domain entry resolves independently — proving the seam is data-driven. **Completion criteria:** prompt selected by domain; coding prompt text identical to current; adding a domain's prompt is a data edit with no selector change; tests green.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.result: System prompt is now DATA keyed by domain: prompts/coding.md (DS builder prompt moved verbatim, sha256 cac64760 byte-identical), domain_prompts.domain_prompt() resolver, device.py -94 lines (SYSTEM_PROMPT = domain_prompt('coding') alias), DS _build_system_prompt resolves by domain, data-driven table seam. 5 new tests green; adding a domain's prompt is now a data edit. Commit bb3af29d.
body.intention: I intend that the system prompt is a property of the task's domain, so a new domain arrives entirely as data (its eligible models + its prompt) with no selector code change.
body.unproven_reason: Behavior-preserving MOVE (byte-identical prompt relocation) has no authentic red->green: the byte-identity characterization test is green at both pre- and post-move states by design (zero behavior change is the point), and proof_emitter rejects the only 'red' available (a fabricated wrong-stub) as non-authentic. Lever needed: a golden-master/characterization proof mode that certifies a pinned invariant holds at HEAD without requiring a failing pre-state (same class as proof_emitter-cant-prove-refactors).
